source_urls.name: Devpost official rules
source_urls.local_relevance: Eligibility, project ownership, team representative, language, IP, testing, and submission rules.

source_urls.name: Devpost overview
source_urls.local_relevance: Challenge scope, deadline, tracks, and public submission entrypoint.

automated_evidence.detail: Local audit states the project was created locally on 2026-06-03 JST during the submission period.

automated_evidence.evidence: README.md, architecture_diagram.md, submission/DEVPOST_SUBMISSION_DRAFT.md, submission/DEVPOST_FIELD_MAP.md, submission/DEMO_VIDEO_SCRIPT.md, submission/VIDEO_RECORDING_RUNBOOK.md, submission/SUBMISSION_REVIEW_QA.md, submission/SUBMISSION_LAUNCH_RUNBOOK.md, submission/JUDGING_ALIGNMENT.md, submission/OFFICIAL_REQUIREMENTS_AUDIT.md
automated_evidence.detail: No Japanese characters found in key public submission materials.
Apache-2.0 license is present and included in the content rights audit.
No bundled audio/video media files are present in the local package.
The package describes demo data as synthetic and avoids private logs/customer data.
The Devpost draft frames a distinct AI incident command and remediation-ledger story.
Final submission remains blocked until public repo and public video URLs are approved and validated.

human_confirmation_items.confirmation_needed: Entrant is at least the age of majority where they reside and is not resident/domiciled in an excluded jurisdiction.
human_confirmation_items.why_codex_cannot_confirm: This depends on personal/legal facts and should be confirmed by the user before Devpost submission.

human_confirmation_items.confirmation_needed: Entrant is not a judge, promotion entity, government/state-owned entity, excluded affiliate, household member, or otherwise conflicted participant.
human_confirmation_items.why_codex_cannot_confirm: This depends on employment, affiliation, household, and conflict facts outside the local packet.

human_confirmation_items.confirmation_needed: If submitted as a team or organization, the submitter is authorized to act as the representative and the team size is within the rules.
human_confirmation_items.why_codex_cannot_confirm: The local packet does not know whether the Devpost entry will be individual, team, or organization.

human_confirmation_items.confirmation_needed: The entrant owns the submitted work or has permission for every included component, and no third-party rights are violated.
human_confirmation_items.why_codex_cannot_confirm: The package can audit files and licenses, but final ownership representation belongs to the entrant.

human_confirmation_items.confirmation_needed: The project was not developed with prohibited financial or preferential support from Sponsor or Administrator.
human_confirmation_items.why_codex_cannot_confirm: This depends on external business, employment, funding, and contract facts.

checks.name: external submit remains gated
checks.detail: user approval and not-submitted wording checked

checks.name: boundary text
checks.detail: This eligibility and compliance audit is local review evidence only. It does not determine legal eligibility, submit Devpost forms, create accounts, publish a repository, upload video, write approved URLs, configure Splunk, or submit anything.

boundary: This eligibility and compliance audit is local review evidence only. It does not determine legal eligibility, submit Devpost forms, create accounts, publish a repository, upload video, write approved URLs, configure Splunk, or submit anything.
recommended_next_step: Before Devpost final submission, the entrant should confirm each human-confirmation item and record approval in the launch runbook or post-action evidence log.
